#!/bin/bash
# в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
# Harness Orchestrator Development Manager
# в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
#
# This script manages development of the @mms/harness-orchestrator package.
# It ensures Claude Code works ONLY within packages/harness-orchestrator/
# and does NOT modify files in the root project (apps/canvas, root features.json).
#
# Usage: ./harness-package-01.sh [command]
#
# DEFAULT: Running without arguments starts Claude with isolation.
# в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

set -e

# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
# CONFIGURATION
# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PACKAGE_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR/packages/harness-orchestrator"
PACKAGE_FEATURES="$PACKAGE_DIR/features.json"
PACKAGE_PROGRESS="$PACKAGE_DIR/progress.txt"
PACKAGE_CLI="$PACKAGE_DIR/dist/cli/index.js"

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
BOLD='\033[1m'
DIM='\033[2m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

print_header() {
    echo -e "\n${BOLD}${MAGENTA}в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ${NC}"
    echo -e "${BOLD}${MAGENTA}  $1${NC}"
    echo -e "${BOLD}${MAGENTA}в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ${NC}\n"
}

print_section() {
    echo -e "\n${CYAN}в–ё $1${NC}"
}

print_success() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}вң“ $1${NC}"
}

print_warning() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}вҡ  $1${NC}"
}

print_error() {
    echo -e "${RED}вң— $1${NC}"
}

print_info() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}в„№ $1${NC}"
}

print_dim() {
    echo -e "${DIM}  $1${NC}"
}

# Validate package exists
check_package() {
    if [[ ! -d "$PACKAGE_DIR" ]]; then
        print_error "Package not found: $PACKAGE_DIR"
        exit 1
    fi
    if [[ ! -f "$PACKAGE_FEATURES" ]]; then
        print_error "Package features.json not found: $PACKAGE_FEATURES"
        exit 1
    fi
}

# Check if CLI is built
check_built() {
    if [[ ! -f "$PACKAGE_CLI" ]]; then
        print_warning "Package not built. Building now..."
        cd "$PACKAGE_DIR"
        bun install
        bun run build
        cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
        print_success "Build complete"
    fi
}

# Count features by status (from package features.json)
count_features() {
    local total=$(jq '.features | length' "$PACKAGE_FEATURES" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    local completed=$(jq '[.features[] | select(.passes == true)] | length' "$PACKAGE_FEATURES" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    local pending=$((total - completed))
    echo "$total $completed $pending"
}

# Get next incomplete feature
get_next_feature() {
    jq -r '.features[] | select(.passes == false) | "\(.id): \(.description)"' "$PACKAGE_FEATURES" 2>/dev/null | head -1
}

# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
# COMMANDS
# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

cmd_help() {
    print_header "рҹ”§ Harness Orchestrator Development Manager"

    echo -e "${BOLD}PURPOSE:${NC}"
    echo "    Manage development of the @mms/harness-orchestrator package."
    echo "    This script ensures Claude Code works ONLY in packages/harness-orchestrator/"
    echo "    and does NOT touch the root project files."
    echo ""

    echo -e "${BOLD}USAGE:${NC}"
    echo "    ./harness-package-01.sh [command]"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BOLD}DEFAULT:${NC}"
    echo "    Running without arguments starts Claude with package isolation."
    echo ""

    echo -e "${BOLD}COMMANDS:${NC}"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}status${NC}        Show package development status"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}next${NC}          Show next feature to implement"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}build${NC}         Build the package (bun run build)"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}dev${NC}           Start TypeScript watch mode"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}test${NC}          Run package tests"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}claude${NC}        Start Claude Code with package isolation"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}log${NC} <msg>     Add note to package progress.txt"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}commit${NC}        Commit package changes only"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}help${NC}          Show this help message"
    echo ""

    echo -e "${BOLD}ISOLATION RULES:${NC}"
    echo -e "    ${RED}вң—${NC} Do NOT modify: apps/canvas/, root features.json, root claude-progress.txt"
    echo -e "    ${GREEN}вң“${NC} Only modify: packages/harness-orchestrator/*"
    echo ""

    echo -e "${BOLD}QUICK START:${NC}"
    echo "    ./harness-package-01.sh           # Start Claude (default)"
    echo "    ./harness-package-01.sh status    # Check package state"
    echo "    ./harness-package-01.sh next      # See next feature"
    echo ""

    echo -e "${BOLD}FILES:${NC}"
    echo "    Package:    packages/harness-orchestrator/"
    echo "    Features:   packages/harness-orchestrator/features.json"
    echo "    Progress:   packages/harness-orchestrator/progress.txt"
    echo "    Docs:       packages/harness-orchestrator/CLAUDE.md"
    echo ""
}

cmd_status() {
    check_package
    print_header "рҹ“Ҡ Harness Orchestrator - Development Status"

    # Package info
    local project=$(jq -r '.project // "harness-orchestrator"' "$PACKAGE_FEATURES")
    local version=$(jq -r '.version // "0.0.0"' "$PACKAGE_FEATURES")
    local updated=$(jq -r '.lastUpdated // "unknown"' "$PACKAGE_FEATURES")

    echo -e "${BOLD}Package:${NC} $project"
    echo -e "${BOLD}Version:${NC} $version"
    echo -e "${BOLD}Updated:${NC} $updated"
    echo -e "${BOLD}Location:${NC} ${DIM}packages/harness-orchestrator/${NC}"
    echo ""

    # Progress stats
    read total completed pending <<< $(count_features)
    local percent=$((completed * 100 / total))

    # Progress bar
    local filled=$((percent / 5))
    local empty=$((20 - filled))
    local bar=""
    for ((i=0; i<filled; i++)); do bar+="в–Ҳ"; done
    for ((i=0; i<empty; i++)); do bar+="в–‘"; done

    echo -e "${BOLD}Progress:${NC} [$bar] $percent%"
    echo -e "  ${GREEN}$completed${NC} completed / ${YELLOW}$pending${NC} pending / $total total"

    # Next feature
    print_section "Next Feature to Implement"
    local next=$(get_next_feature)
    if [[ -n "$next" ]]; then
        echo -e "  ${CYAN}$next${NC}"
    else
        print_success "All features complete!"
    fi

    # Recent progress
    if [[ -f "$PACKAGE_PROGRESS" ]]; then
        print_section "Recent Sessions (from progress.txt)"
        grep -E "^## Session|^## O[0-9]" "$PACKAGE_PROGRESS" 2>/dev/null | tail -5 | while read line; do
            echo -e "  ${DIM}$line${NC}"
        done
    fi

    # Isolation reminder
    print_section "Isolation Reminder"
    echo -e "  ${RED}вң—${NC} Do NOT modify: apps/, root features.json, root CLAUDE.md"
    echo -e "  ${GREEN}вң“${NC} Only modify: packages/harness-orchestrator/*"
    echo ""
}

cmd_next() {
    check_package
    print_header "рҹҺҜ Next Feature to Implement"

    # Find first incomplete feature
    local feature=$(jq -r '.features[] | select(.passes == false) | . | @json' "$PACKAGE_FEATURES" 2>/dev/null | head -1)

    if [[ -z "$feature" ]]; then
        print_success "All features are complete! рҹҺү"
        exit 0
    fi

    # Parse feature details
    local id=$(echo "$feature" | jq -r '.id')
    local desc=$(echo "$feature" | jq -r '.description')
    local category=$(echo "$feature" | jq -r '.category // "general"')
    local blocked=$(echo "$feature" | jq -r '.blockedBy // empty')
    local notes=$(echo "$feature" | jq -r '.notes // empty')

    echo -e "${BOLD}Feature:${NC} ${CYAN}$id${NC}"
    echo -e "${BOLD}Description:${NC} $desc"
    echo -e "${BOLD}Category:${NC} $category"

    if [[ -n "$blocked" && "$blocked" != "null" ]]; then
        echo -e "${BOLD}Blocked By:${NC} $blocked"
    fi

    # Show steps if available
    local steps=$(echo "$feature" | jq -r '.steps // empty')
    if [[ -n "$steps" && "$steps" != "null" && "$steps" != "[]" ]]; then
        echo ""
        echo -e "${BOLD}Steps:${NC}"
        echo "$feature" | jq -r '.steps[]' 2>/dev/null | nl -w2 -s'. '
    fi

    if [[ -n "$notes" && "$notes" != "null" ]]; then
        echo ""
        echo -e "${BOLD}Notes:${NC}"
        echo "  $notes"
    fi

    echo ""
    echo -e "${DIM}Run: ./harness-package-01.sh${NC}"
}

cmd_build() {
    check_package
    print_header "рҹ”Ё Building Package"

    cd "$PACKAGE_DIR"

    print_section "Installing dependencies"
    bun install

    print_section "Compiling TypeScript"
    bun run build

    print_success "Build complete!"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${DIM}Output: packages/harness-orchestrator/dist/${NC}"

    cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
}

cmd_dev() {
    check_package
    print_header "рҹ‘Җ Starting Watch Mode"

    echo -e "${DIM}Press Ctrl+C to stop${NC}"
    echo ""

    cd "$PACKAGE_DIR"
    bun run dev
}

cmd_test() {
    check_package
    check_built
    print_header "рҹ§Ә Testing Package CLI"

    print_section "Testing 'harness status'"
    node "$PACKAGE_CLI" status "$PACKAGE_FEATURES"

    print_section "Testing 'harness analyze'"
    node "$PACKAGE_CLI" analyze "$PACKAGE_FEATURES"

    print_success "CLI tests passed!"
}

cmd_log() {
    check_package

    if [[ -z "$1" ]]; then
        print_error "Usage: ./harness-package-01.sh log <message>"
        exit 1
    fi

    local timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    local message="- [$timestamp] $1"

    echo "$message" >> "$PACKAGE_PROGRESS"

    print_success "Added to progress.txt:"
    echo -e "  ${DIM}$message${NC}"
}

cmd_commit() {
    check_package
    print_header "рҹ“Ұ Commit Package Changes"

    cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

    # Show only package changes
    print_section "Package Changes (packages/harness-orchestrator/)"
    git status --short packages/harness-orchestrator/ 2>/dev/null || echo "  (no changes)"

    # Warn about root changes
    print_section "Root Changes (should be empty!)"
    local root_changes=$(git status --short --porcelain | grep -v "^.. packages/harness-orchestrator" | grep -v "^.. harness-manager.sh" | head -10)
    if [[ -n "$root_changes" ]]; then
        print_warning "Found changes outside the package:"
        echo "$root_changes" | while read line; do
            echo -e "  ${RED}$line${NC}"
        done
        echo ""
        print_warning "These files should NOT be committed with package changes!"
        echo ""
    else
        print_success "No root changes detected"
    fi

    # Generate commit command
    print_section "Commit Command"
    echo -e "${DIM}Run manually after review:${NC}"
    echo ""
    echo "  git add packages/harness-orchestrator/"
    echo "  git commit -m \"feat(orchestrator): <description>\""
    echo ""
}

cmd_claude() {
    check_package
    print_header "рҹӨ– Starting Claude Code (Package Isolated)"

    # Get next feature for context
    local next=$(get_next_feature)
    read total completed pending <<< $(count_features)

    echo -e "${BOLD}Package:${NC} @mms/harness-orchestrator"
    echo -e "${BOLD}Progress:${NC} $completed/$total features complete"
    echo -e "${BOLD}Next:${NC} ${next:-"All features complete!"}"
    echo ""

    # Build isolation prompt
    local isolation_prompt="You are working on the @mms/harness-orchestrator package.

    # Change to package directory so Claude's context is focused there
    cd "$PACKAGE_DIR"
    claude --append-system-prompt="$isolation_prompt"
}

cmd_quick() {
    # One-liner for shell prompt integration
    check_package 2>/dev/null || { echo "вҡ  no pkg"; exit 0; }
    read total completed pending <<< $(count_features)
    local percent=$((completed * 100 / total))
    echo "рҹ”§ orchestrator: $completed/$total ($percent%)"
}

# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
# MAIN
# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

main() {
    local command="${1:-help}"
    shift 2>/dev/null || true

    case "$command" in
        status|s)
            cmd_status
            ;;
        next|n)
            cmd_next
            ;;
        build|b)
            cmd_build
            ;;
        dev|d)
            cmd_dev
            ;;
        test|t)
            cmd_test
            ;;
        claude|c)
            cmd_claude
            ;;
        log|l)
            cmd_log "$@"
            ;;
        commit)
            cmd_commit
            ;;
        quick|q)
            cmd_quick
            ;;
        help|h|--help|-h)
            cmd_help
            ;;
        *)
            print_error "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Run './harness-package-01.sh help' for usage"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
}

main "$@"

#!/bin/bash
# в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
# MMS Root Harness Development Manager
# в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
#
# This script manages development of the MMS root project (apps/canvas).
# It ensures Claude Code works on the ROOT project and ignores packages
# that have their own harness (features.json + progress.txt).
#
# Usage: ./harness-root.sh [command] [options]
# в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

set -e

# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
# CONFIGURATION
# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
ROOT_FEATURES="$SCRIPT_DIR/features.json"
ROOT_PROGRESS="$SCRIPT_DIR/claude-progress.txt"
APP_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR/apps/canvas"

# Packages with their own harness (auto-detected)
ISOLATED_PACKAGES=()

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
BOLD='\033[1m'
DIM='\033[2m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

print_header() {
    echo -e "\n${BOLD}${BLUE}в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ${NC}"
    echo -e "${BOLD}${BLUE}  $1${NC}"
    echo -e "${BOLD}${BLUE}в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ${NC}\n"
}

print_section() {
    echo -e "\n${CYAN}в–ё $1${NC}"
}

print_success() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}вң“ $1${NC}"
}

print_warning() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}вҡ  $1${NC}"
}

print_error() {
    echo -e "${RED}вң— $1${NC}"
}

print_info() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}в„№ $1${NC}"
}

print_dim() {
    echo -e "${DIM}  $1${NC}"
}

# Detect packages with their own harness (features.json)
detect_isolated_packages() {
    ISOLATED_PACKAGES=()
    while IFS= read -r features_file; do
        local pkg_dir=$(dirname "$features_file")
        local pkg_name=$(basename "$pkg_dir")
        ISOLATED_PACKAGES+=("$pkg_name")
    done < <(find "$SCRIPT_DIR/packages" -maxdepth 2 -name "features.json" 2>/dev/null)
}

# List isolated packages for display
list_isolated_packages() {
    detect_isolated_packages
    if [[ ${#ISOLATED_PACKAGES[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        echo "  (none)"
    else
        for pkg in "${ISOLATED_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
            echo -e "  ${DIM}packages/$pkg/${NC}"
        done
    fi
}

# Generate exclude pattern for git commands
get_exclude_pattern() {
    detect_isolated_packages
    local pattern=""
    for pkg in "${ISOLATED_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
        pattern+=" ':!packages/$pkg'"
    done
    echo "$pattern"
}

# Validate root files exist
check_root() {
    if [[ ! -f "$ROOT_FEATURES" ]]; then
        print_error "Root features.json not found: $ROOT_FEATURES"
        exit 1
    fi
    if [[ ! -d "$APP_DIR" ]]; then
        print_error "App directory not found: $APP_DIR"
        exit 1
    fi
}

# Count features by status (from root features.json)
count_features() {
    local total=$(jq '.features | length' "$ROOT_FEATURES" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    local completed=$(jq '[.features[] | select(.passes == true)] | length' "$ROOT_FEATURES" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    local pending=$((total - completed))
    echo "$total $completed $pending"
}

# Get next incomplete feature
get_next_feature() {
    jq -r '.features[] | select(.passes == false) | "\(.id): \(.description)"' "$ROOT_FEATURES" 2>/dev/null | head -1
}

# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
# COMMANDS
# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

cmd_help() {
    print_header "рҹҸ  MMS Root Harness Development Manager"

    echo -e "${BOLD}PURPOSE:${NC}"
    echo "    Manage development of the MMS root project (apps/canvas)."
    echo "    This script ensures Claude Code works on the ROOT project"
    echo "    and ignores packages with their own harness."
    echo ""

    echo -e "${BOLD}USAGE:${NC}"
    echo "    ./harness-root.sh [command] [options]"
    echo ""

    echo -e "${BOLD}COMMANDS:${NC}"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}status${NC}        Show root project development status"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}next${NC}          Show next feature to implement"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}dev${NC}           Start development server (apps/canvas)"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}build${NC}         Build the app"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}typecheck${NC}     Run TypeScript type checking"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}claude${NC}        Start Claude Code with root isolation"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}log${NC} <msg>     Add note to claude-progress.txt"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}commit${NC}        Commit root changes (excludes isolated packages)"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}packages${NC}      List packages with their own harness"
    echo -e "    ${CYAN}help${NC}          Show this help message"
    echo ""

    echo -e "${BOLD}SCOPE RULES:${NC}"
    echo -e "    ${GREEN}вң“${NC} Work on: apps/canvas/, root files, packages/toolkit/"
    echo -e "    ${RED}вң—${NC} Ignore: packages with own harness (auto-detected)"
    echo ""

    echo -e "${BOLD}QUICK START:${NC}"
    echo "    1. ./harness-root.sh status    # Check project state"
    echo "    2. ./harness-root.sh next      # See next feature"
    echo "    3. ./harness-root.sh claude    # Start Claude (root scope)"
    echo ""

    echo -e "${BOLD}FILES:${NC}"
    echo "    App:        apps/canvas/"
    echo "    Features:   features.json"
    echo "    Progress:   claude-progress.txt"
    echo "    Docs:       CLAUDE.md"
    echo ""
}

cmd_status() {
    check_root
    detect_isolated_packages
    print_header "рҹ“Ҡ MMS Root Project - Development Status"

    # Project info
    local project=$(jq -r '.project // "MMS"' "$ROOT_FEATURES")
    local version=$(jq -r '.version // "0.0.0"' "$ROOT_FEATURES")
    local updated=$(jq -r '.lastUpdated // "unknown"' "$ROOT_FEATURES")

    echo -e "${BOLD}Project:${NC} $project"
    echo -e "${BOLD}Version:${NC} $version"
    echo -e "${BOLD}Updated:${NC} $updated"
    echo -e "${BOLD}App:${NC} ${DIM}apps/canvas/${NC}"
    echo ""

    # Progress stats
    read total completed pending <<< $(count_features)
    local percent=0
    if [[ $total -gt 0 ]]; then
        percent=$((completed * 100 / total))
    fi

    # Progress bar
    local filled=$((percent / 5))
    local empty=$((20 - filled))
    local bar=""
    for ((i=0; i<filled; i++)); do bar+="в–Ҳ"; done
    for ((i=0; i<empty; i++)); do bar+="в–‘"; done

    echo -e "${BOLD}Progress:${NC} [$bar] $percent%"
    echo -e "  ${GREEN}$completed${NC} completed / ${YELLOW}$pending${NC} pending / $total total"

    # Next feature
    print_section "Next Feature to Implement"
    local next=$(get_next_feature)
    if [[ -n "$next" ]]; then
        echo -e "  ${CYAN}$next${NC}"
    else
        print_success "All features complete!"
    fi

    # Isolated packages
    print_section "Packages with Own Harness (Ignored)"
    if [[ ${#ISOLATED_PACKAGES[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        echo "  (none detected)"
    else
        for pkg in "${ISOLATED_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
            local pkg_features="$SCRIPT_DIR/packages/$pkg/features.json"
            local pkg_total=$(jq '.features | length' "$pkg_features" 2>/dev/null || echo "?")
            local pkg_done=$(jq '[.features[] | select(.passes == true)] | length' "$pkg_features" 2>/dev/null || echo "?")
            echo -e "  ${DIM}packages/$pkg/${NC} ($pkg_done/$pkg_total features)"
        done
        echo ""
        echo -e "  ${DIM}Use ./harness-package-01.sh to work on isolated packages${NC}"
    fi

    # Recent progress
    if [[ -f "$ROOT_PROGRESS" ]]; then
        print_section "Recent Sessions (from claude-progress.txt)"
        grep -E "^## Session|^## O[0-9]" "$ROOT_PROGRESS" 2>/dev/null | tail -5 | while read line; do
            echo -e "  ${DIM}$line${NC}"
        done
    fi

    echo ""
}

cmd_next() {
    check_root
    print_header "рҹҺҜ Next Feature to Implement"

    # Find first incomplete feature
    local feature=$(jq -r '.features[] | select(.passes == false) | . | @json' "$ROOT_FEATURES" 2>/dev/null | head -1)

    if [[ -z "$feature" ]]; then
        print_success "All features are complete! рҹҺү"
        exit 0
    fi

    # Parse feature details
    local id=$(echo "$feature" | jq -r '.id')
    local desc=$(echo "$feature" | jq -r '.description')
    local category=$(echo "$feature" | jq -r '.category // "general"')
    local blocked=$(echo "$feature" | jq -r '.blockedBy // empty')
    local notes=$(echo "$feature" | jq -r '.notes // empty')
    local priority=$(echo "$feature" | jq -r '.priority // 99')

    echo -e "${BOLD}Feature:${NC} ${CYAN}$id${NC}"
    echo -e "${BOLD}Description:${NC} $desc"
    echo -e "${BOLD}Category:${NC} $category"
    echo -e "${BOLD}Priority:${NC} $priority"

    if [[ -n "$blocked" && "$blocked" != "null" ]]; then
        echo -e "${BOLD}Blocked By:${NC} $blocked"
    fi

    # Show steps if available
    local steps=$(echo "$feature" | jq -r '.steps // empty')
    if [[ -n "$steps" && "$steps" != "null" && "$steps" != "[]" ]]; then
        echo ""
        echo -e "${BOLD}Steps:${NC}"
        echo "$feature" | jq -r '.steps[]' 2>/dev/null | nl -w2 -s'. '
    fi

    if [[ -n "$notes" && "$notes" != "null" ]]; then
        echo ""
        echo -e "${BOLD}Notes:${NC}"
        echo "  $notes"
    fi

    echo ""
    echo -e "${DIM}Run: ./harness-root.sh claude${NC}"
}

cmd_dev() {
    check_root
    print_header "рҹҡҖ Starting Development Server"

    echo -e "${DIM}Starting apps/canvas at http://localhost:5173${NC}"
    echo -e "${DIM}Press Ctrl+C to stop${NC}"
    echo ""

    cd "$APP_DIR"
    bun run dev
}

cmd_build() {
    check_root
    print_header "рҹ”Ё Building App"

    cd "$APP_DIR"

    print_section "Installing dependencies"
    bun install

    print_section "Building app"
    bun run build

    print_success "Build complete!"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${DIM}Output: apps/canvas/dist/${NC}"

    cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
}

cmd_typecheck() {
    check_root
    print_header "рҹ”Қ Type Checking"

    cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
    bun run typecheck

    print_success "Type check complete!"
}

cmd_log() {
    check_root

    if [[ -z "$1" ]]; then
        print_error "Usage: ./harness-root.sh log <message>"
        exit 1
    fi

    local timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    local message="- [$timestamp] $1"

    echo "$message" >> "$ROOT_PROGRESS"

    print_success "Added to claude-progress.txt:"
    echo -e "  ${DIM}$message${NC}"
}

cmd_commit() {
    check_root
    detect_isolated_packages
    print_header "рҹ“Ұ Commit Root Changes"

    cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

    # Build exclude args for git
    local exclude_args=""
    for pkg in "${ISOLATED_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
        exclude_args+=" -- . ':!packages/$pkg'"
    done

    # Show root changes (excluding isolated packages)
    print_section "Root Changes (to be committed)"
    if [[ ${#ISOLATED_PACKAGES[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        eval "git diff --stat HEAD $exclude_args" 2>/dev/null || echo "  (no changes)"
    else
        git diff --stat HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "  (no changes)"
    fi

    # Warn about isolated package changes
    print_section "Isolated Package Changes (will NOT be committed)"
    for pkg in "${ISOLATED_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
        local pkg_changes=$(git status --short "packages/$pkg" 2>/dev/null)
        if [[ -n "$pkg_changes" ]]; then
            echo -e "  ${YELLOW}packages/$pkg/:${NC}"
            echo "$pkg_changes" | while read line; do
                echo -e "    ${DIM}$line${NC}"
            done
        fi
    done

    # Generate commit command
    print_section "Commit Command"
    echo -e "${DIM}Run manually after review:${NC}"
    echo ""

    if [[ ${#ISOLATED_PACKAGES[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        echo "  # Stage root changes only (excludes isolated packages)"
        echo "  git add ."
        for pkg in "${ISOLATED_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
            echo "  git reset HEAD packages/$pkg/"
        done
    else
        echo "  git add ."
    fi
    echo "  git commit -m \"feat(canvas): <description>\""
    echo ""
}

cmd_packages() {
    detect_isolated_packages
    print_header "рҹ“Ұ Packages with Own Harness"

    if [[ ${#ISOLATED_PACKAGES[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        print_info "No packages with their own harness detected."
        echo ""
        echo -e "${DIM}A package has its own harness if it contains:${NC}"
        echo -e "${DIM}  - features.json${NC}"
        echo -e "${DIM}  - progress.txt (optional)${NC}"
        exit 0
    fi

    echo -e "${BOLD}These packages have their own harness and are IGNORED by root:${NC}"
    echo ""

    for pkg in "${ISOLATED_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
        local pkg_dir="$SCRIPT_DIR/packages/$pkg"
        local pkg_features="$pkg_dir/features.json"

        local pkg_total=$(jq '.features | length' "$pkg_features" 2>/dev/null || echo "?")
        local pkg_done=$(jq '[.features[] | select(.passes == true)] | length' "$pkg_features" 2>/dev/null || echo "?")
        local pkg_desc=$(jq -r '.project // .description // "No description"' "$pkg_features" 2>/dev/null | head -c 50)

        echo -e "  ${CYAN}$pkg${NC}"
        echo -e "    Path:     packages/$pkg/"
        echo -e "    Progress: $pkg_done/$pkg_total features"
        echo -e "    Desc:     ${DIM}$pkg_desc${NC}"
        echo ""
    done

    echo -e "${DIM}To work on an isolated package, use its dedicated harness script.${NC}"
    echo -e "${DIM}Example: ./harness-package-01.sh claude${NC}"
    echo ""
}

cmd_claude() {
    check_root
    detect_isolated_packages
    print_header "рҹӨ– Starting Claude Code (Root Scope)"

    # Get next feature for context
    local next=$(get_next_feature)
    read total completed pending <<< $(count_features)

    echo -e "${BOLD}Project:${NC} MMS Root (apps/canvas)"
    echo -e "${BOLD}Progress:${NC} $completed/$total features complete"
    echo -e "${BOLD}Next:${NC} ${next:-"All features complete!"}"
    echo ""

    # Build isolated packages list for prompt
    local isolated_list=""
    for pkg in "${ISOLATED_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
        isolated_list+="   - packages/$pkg/ (has own harness)
"
    done

    # Build isolation prompt
    local isolation_prompt="You are working on the MMS ROOT project (apps/canvas).

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹҸ  ROOT SCOPE RULES - READ CAREFULLY
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

вң… YOU MAY MODIFY:

рҹ“Ӣ ROOT HARNESS PROTOCOL
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

1. READ claude-progress.txt вҶ’ current state
2. READ features.json вҶ’ find next feature (passes: false)
3. READ CLAUDE.md вҶ’ project instructions

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“Ҡ CURRENT STATUS
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

    echo -e "${DIM}$isolation_prompt${NC}" | head -35
    echo -e "${DIM}... (truncated for display)${NC}"

    echo ""

    print_info "Starting Claude Code with root scope..."
    echo ""

    # Start Claude with isolation prompt (stay in root)
    cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
    claude --append-system-prompt="$isolation_prompt"
}

cmd_quick() {
    # One-liner for shell prompt integration
    check_root 2>/dev/null || { echo "вҡ  no root"; exit 0; }
    read total completed pending <<< $(count_features)
    local percent=0
    if [[ $total -gt 0 ]]; then
        percent=$((completed * 100 / total))
    fi
    echo "рҹҸ  mms-root: $completed/$total ($percent%)"
}

# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
# MAIN
# в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

main() {
    local command="${1:-help}"
    shift 2>/dev/null || true

    case "$command" in
        status|s)
            cmd_status
            ;;
        next|n)
            cmd_next
            ;;
        dev|d)
            cmd_dev
            ;;
        build|b)
            cmd_build
            ;;
        typecheck|tc)
            cmd_typecheck
            ;;
        claude|c)
            cmd_claude
            ;;
        log|l)
            cmd_log "$@"
            ;;
        commit)
            cmd_commit
            ;;
        packages|p)
            cmd_packages
            ;;
        quick|q)
            cmd_quick
            ;;
        help|h|--help|-h)
            cmd_help
            ;;
        *)
            print_error "Unknown command: $command"
            echo "Run './harness-root.sh help' for usage"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
}

main "$@"

[1mFeature:[0m [0;36mO022-B[0m
[1mDescription:[0m KnowledgeManager: Manage .harness/kb/ knowledge base for AI verification
[1mCategory:[0m doctor
[1mBlocked By:[0m O022-A

[1mSteps:[0m
 1. 1. CREATE DIRECTORY: packages/harness-orchestrator/knowledge/ with README.md explaining purpose
 2. 2. CREATE DIRECTORY: packages/harness-orchestrator/templates/kb/ for files that get copied to projects
 3. 3. CREATE FILE: templates/kb/harness-guide.xml - XML file with Anthropic harness best practices (extract key concepts from https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
 4. 4. CREATE FILE: templates/kb/best-practices.xml - XML file with Claude Code best practices (extract from https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices)
 5. 5. CREATE FILE: templates/kb/manifest.json - { version: '1.0.0', updated: 'YYYY-MM-DD', files: [...] }
 6. 6. CREATE FILE: src/doctor/KnowledgeManager.ts with class KnowledgeManager
 7. 7. IMPLEMENT constructor(projectPath: string) that sets this.kbPath = join(projectPath, '.harness', 'kb')
 8. 8. IMPLEMENT exists(): Promise<boolean> - check if .harness/kb/ exists
 9. 9. IMPLEMENT setup(): Promise<void> - copy templates/kb/* to project .harness/kb/, create .harness/ if needed
10. 10. IMPLEMENT getVersion(): Promise<string> - read manifest.json version
11. 11. IMPLEMENT needsUpdate(): Promise<boolean> - compare project KB version with bundled version
12. 12. IMPLEMENT readForAI(): Promise<string> - concatenate all XML files into single string for AI context
13. 13. ADD CLI: 'harness doctor --setup-kb' option that calls KnowledgeManager.setup() and reports success
14. 14. XML FORMAT: Use Repomix-style XML: <?xml version="1.0"?><knowledge-base version="1.0"><document id="..."><title>...</title><section id="..."><content>...</content></section></document></knowledge-base>

[1mNotes:[0m
  CONTEXT: The Knowledge Base (.harness/kb/) provides AI with context about harness best practices. When AI checks run (O022-D, O022-E), they read these XML files to understand what a well-structured harness looks like. The XML format is inspired by Repomix which is optimized for Claude context. KEY INSIGHT: The KB is stored PER PROJECT in .harness/kb/ so each project can have customized knowledge. REFERENCE: See https://repomix.com/guide/output for XML format inspiration.

[2mRun: ./harness-package-01.sh[0m

content.text:
HГЎ um erro. O comando `claude --append-system-prompt` nГЈo funciona corretamente em modo nГЈo-interativo. Preciso verificar como o comando claude Г© invocado e corrigir. O problema Г© que estou testando via script que nГЈo Г© interativo.

Vou verificar se o erro ocorre apenas no contexto de teste ou se Г© um problema real:

content.content:
/Users/anotonio.silva/.nvm/versions/node/v24.11.1/bin/claude
  --append-system-prompt <prompt>                   Append a system prompt to the default system prompt
  --permission-mode <mode>                          Permission mode to use for the session (choices: "acceptEdits", "bypassPermissions", "default", "delegate", "dontAsk", "plan")
  -c, --continue                                    Continue the most recent conversation

content.text:
O `--append-system-prompt` existe. O erro acontece porque estou rodando via pipe/script que captura a saГӯda. Em uso real (terminal interativo), o comando funcionaria.

O teste real precisa ser em terminal interativo. O script estГЎ correto - o erro Г© do contexto de teste. 

O script estГЎ pronto. Quando vocГӘ rodar `./harness-package-01.sh` diretamente no terminal, ele vai:
1. Mostrar o status
2. Mostrar o prompt de isolamento
3. Iniciar o Claude Code com o prompt anexado

Quer que eu teste de outra forma ou considera o `harness-package-01.sh` concluГӯdo?

Here are the search results for `--append-system-prompt` usage in Claude Code CLI:

## Overview

The `--append-system-prompt` flag is used when you want to add specific instructions while keeping Claude Code's default capabilities intact.(no content)This is the safest option for most use cases, as it preserves Claude Code's built-in capabilities while adding your custom requirements.(no content)The `--append-system-prompt` option adds your custom instructions to Claude's existing helpful behavior rather than replacing it entirely. As of v1.0.51, `--append-system-prompt` works in interactive mode, not just print mode.

## Usage Examples

### Basic Print Mode Examples

```bash
claude -p "Analyze these errors" \
  --append-system-prompt "You are an SRE expert" \
  --output-format json \
  --allowedTools "Bash,Read,mcp__datadog"
```(no content)```bash
gh pr diff 123 | claude -p \
  --append-system-prompt "You are a security engineer" \
  --output-format json \
  --allowedTools "Read,Grep" > audit.json
```

### Security-Focused Code Review
```bash
claude -p "Review this authentication code" --append-system-prompt "After analyzing the code, always check for common security vulnerabilities like SQL injection, XSS, and insecure token handling."
```

### Database Expertise
```bash
claude -p "Design a user system" --append-system-prompt "You are a database architect with 10 years PostgreSQL experience. Focus on proper normalization, indexing strategies, and query performance. Always consider data integrity and scalability."
```

### Interactive Mode Examples

```bash
# Interactive mode with algorithm performance focus
claude --append-system-prompt "When writing code, prioritize algorithm efficiency and performance optimization. Always analyze time and space complexity and suggest performance improvements."
```(no content)```bash
claude --append-system-prompt "You are conducting a senior-level code review. Focus on: 1) Performance bottlenecks, 2) Security vulnerabilities, 3) Code maintainability, 4) Test coverage gaps. Provide specific line-by-line feedback with improvement suggestions."
```

### Legacy Code Modernization
```bash
claude --append-system-prompt "You are modernizing legacy code. Prioritize backwards compatibility while introducing modern patterns. Always explain migration strategies and potential breaking changes."
```

## Related Options

You can also use `--system-prompt-file` to load a custom prompt from a file, which is useful for team consistency or version-controlled prompt templates.(no content)`--system-prompt` and `--system-prompt-file` are mutually exclusive - you cannot use both flags simultaneously.(no content)Use `--system-prompt` or `--system-prompt-file` only when you need complete control over the system prompt.

Here are the search results for `--append-system-prompt` usage in Claude Code CLI:

## Overview

The `--append-system-prompt` flag is used when you want to add specific instructions while keeping Claude Code's default capabilities intact.(no content)This is the safest option for most use cases, as it preserves Claude Code's built-in capabilities while adding your custom requirements.(no content)The `--append-system-prompt` option adds your custom instructions to Claude's existing helpful behavior rather than replacing it entirely. As of v1.0.51, `--append-system-prompt` works in interactive mode, not just print mode.

## Usage Examples

### Basic Print Mode Examples

```bash
claude -p "Analyze these errors" \
  --append-system-prompt "You are an SRE expert" \
  --output-format json \
  --allowedTools "Bash,Read,mcp__datadog"
```(no content)```bash
gh pr diff 123 | claude -p \
  --append-system-prompt "You are a security engineer" \
  --output-format json \
  --allowedTools "Read,Grep" > audit.json
```

### Security-Focused Code Review
```bash
claude -p "Review this authentication code" --append-system-prompt "After analyzing the code, always check for common security vulnerabilities like SQL injection, XSS, and insecure token handling."
```

### Database Expertise
```bash
claude -p "Design a user system" --append-system-prompt "You are a database architect with 10 years PostgreSQL experience. Focus on proper normalization, indexing strategies, and query performance. Always consider data integrity and scalability."
```

### Interactive Mode Examples

```bash
# Interactive mode with algorithm performance focus
claude --append-system-prompt "When writing code, prioritize algorithm efficiency and performance optimization. Always analyze time and space complexity and suggest performance improvements."
```(no content)```bash
claude --append-system-prompt "You are conducting a senior-level code review. Focus on: 1) Performance bottlenecks, 2) Security vulnerabilities, 3) Code maintainability, 4) Test coverage gaps. Provide specific line-by-line feedback with improvement suggestions."
```

### Legacy Code Modernization
```bash
claude --append-system-prompt "You are modernizing legacy code. Prioritize backwards compatibility while introducing modern patterns. Always explain migration strategies and potential breaking changes."
```

## Related Options

You can also use `--system-prompt-file` to load a custom prompt from a file, which is useful for team consistency or version-controlled prompt templates.(no content)`--system-prompt` and `--system-prompt-file` are mutually exclusive - you cannot use both flags simultaneously.(no content)Use `--system-prompt` or `--system-prompt-file` only when you need complete control over the system prompt.

content.content:
# --append-system-prompt Flag Documentation

## Overview
The `--append-system-prompt` flag allows you to **append custom text to the end of the default system prompt** while preserving Claude Code's built-in capabilities.

## Key Characteristics

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Modes** | Interactive + Print |
| **Behavior** | Appends to default prompt (non-destructive) |
| **Use Case** | Add specific instructions while keeping Claude Code behavior intact |
| **Recommendation** | Safest option for most use cases |

## Usage Examples

### Basic Interactive Mode
```bash
claude --append-system-prompt "Always use TypeScript"
```

### With Additional Instructions
```bash
claude --append-system-prompt "Always use TypeScript and include JSDoc comments"
```

### Print Mode
```bash
claude -p --append-system-prompt "Always use TypeScript" "query"
```

## When to Use Each System Prompt Flag

| Flag | Behavior | When to Use |
|------|----------|------------|
| `--append-system-prompt` | **Appends** to default | Add specific instructions while keeping defaults вң“ RECOMMENDED |
| `--system-prompt` | **Replaces** entire prompt | Need complete control over behavior |
| `--system-prompt-file` | **Replaces** with file contents | Load prompts from version-controlled files |

## Key Recommendations

вң… **Use `--append-system-prompt`** for most cases because it:
- Preserves Claude Code's default capabilities
- Adds your custom requirements safely
- Works in both interactive and print modes

вқҢ **Avoid replacing the system prompt** unless you need complete control and understand you're removing all default Claude Code instructions.

**Note:** `--system-prompt` and `--system-prompt-file` are mutually exclusive and cannot be used together simultaneously.

# Plano: Corrigir harness-package-01.sh para modo interativo

## Problema Identificado

O script `harness-package-01.sh` usa `claude --append-system-prompt="..."` que:
- вң… Funciona em terminal interativo (TTY)
- вқҢ Falha quando nГЈo hГЎ TTY (pipes, scripts, etc.)

O erro:
```
Error: Input must be provided either through stdin or as a prompt argument when using --print
```

## Causa Raiz

Segundo a [documentaГ§ГЈo do Claude Code CLI](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/cli-reference):
- `--append-system-prompt` funciona em modo interativo E print
- Modo interativo requer TTY
- Quando nГЈo hГЎ TTY, Claude assume modo print e espera uma query

## SoluГ§ГЈo

Adicionar verificaГ§ГЈo de TTY antes de iniciar Claude:

```bash
# Verificar se estГЎ em terminal interativo
if [[ ! -t 0 ]] || [[ ! -t 1 ]]; then
    print_error "This command requires an interactive terminal."
    print_info "Run directly in terminal, not via pipe or script."
    exit 1
fi
```

## Arquivo a Modificar

- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/harness-package-01.sh`
  - FunГ§ГЈo `cmd_claude()` (linha ~348-425)
  - Adicionar verificaГ§ГЈo de TTY antes de `cd "$PACKAGE_DIR"` e `claude ...`

## BenefГӯcios

1. Mensagem de erro clara quando nГЈo hГЎ TTY
2. Orienta o usuГЎrio a rodar diretamente no terminal
3. Script falha graciosamente em vez de erro confuso do Claude CLI

## Teste

ApГіs implementaГ§ГЈo:
1. `./harness-package-01.sh 2>&1 | head` вҶ’ deve mostrar erro amigГЎvel
2. Rodar diretamente no terminal вҶ’ deve iniciar Claude

# AnГЎlise: harness-package-01.sh - NГЈo hГЎ falha

## SituaГ§ГЈo Real

**O script estГЎ correto.** O "erro" que apareceu foi causado pela forma como testei:

```bash
./harness-package-01.sh 2>&1 | head -50  # вҶҗ ERRADO: pipe remove TTY
```

## Por que o erro apareceu

1. Quando uso `| head -50`, o stdout Г© redirecionado para um pipe
2. Pipes nГЈo sГЈo TTY (terminal)
3. Claude CLI detecta que nГЈo hГЎ TTY e assume modo `--print`
4. Modo `--print` requer uma query como argumento
5. Como nГЈo passei query, deu erro

## Comportamento Correto

Quando vocГӘ rodar diretamente no terminal:
```bash
./harness-package-01.sh  # вҶҗ CORRETO: terminal interativo
```

O Claude iniciarГЎ normalmente com o prompt de isolamento anexado.

## ConclusГЈo

**NГЈo hГЎ nada a corrigir.** O script funciona corretamente em uso real.

# Plano: Corrigir harness-package-01.sh para modo interativo

## Problema Identificado

O script `harness-package-01.sh` usa `claude --append-system-prompt="..."` que:
- вң… Funciona em terminal interativo (TTY)
- вқҢ Falha quando nГЈo hГЎ TTY (pipes, scripts, etc.)

O erro:
```
Error: Input must be provided either through stdin or as a prompt argument when using --print
```

## Causa Raiz

Segundo a [documentaГ§ГЈo do Claude Code CLI](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/cli-reference):
- `--append-system-prompt` funciona em modo interativo E print
- Modo interativo requer TTY
- Quando nГЈo hГЎ TTY, Claude assume modo print e espera uma query

## SoluГ§ГЈo

Adicionar verificaГ§ГЈo de TTY antes de iniciar Claude:

```bash
# Verificar se estГЎ em terminal interativo
if [[ ! -t 0 ]] || [[ ! -t 1 ]]; then
    print_error "This command requires an interactive terminal."
    print_info "Run directly in terminal, not via pipe or script."
    exit 1
fi
```

## Arquivo a Modificar

- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/harness-package-01.sh`
  - FunГ§ГЈo `cmd_claude()` (linha ~348-425)
  - Adicionar verificaГ§ГЈo de TTY antes de `cd "$PACKAGE_DIR"` e `claude ...`

## BenefГӯcios

1. Mensagem de erro clara quando nГЈo hГЎ TTY
2. Orienta o usuГЎrio a rodar diretamente no terminal
3. Script falha graciosamente em vez de erro confuso do Claude CLI

## Teste

ApГіs implementaГ§ГЈo:
1. `./harness-package-01.sh 2>&1 | head` вҶ’ deve mostrar erro amigГЎvel
2. Rodar diretamente no terminal вҶ’ deve iniciar Claude

content.text:
Excelentes perguntas! Deixe-me repensar do zero...

**Brainstorming:**

1. **O script PRECISA iniciar Claude interativamente?**
   - Atualmente: sim, usa `claude --append-system-prompt="..."`
   - Alternativa: poderia usar `claude -p` (print mode) com uma query inicial?

2. **Qual Г© o objetivo real do harness?**
   - Garantir que Claude trabalhe isolado em um package
   - Passar contexto sobre a prГіxima tarefa
   - Automatizar o fluxo: ler progress вҶ’ ler features вҶ’ implementar вҶ’ commitar

3. **E se o script NГғO precisasse iniciar Claude?**
   - O script poderia apenas **preparar o ambiente** e **exibir instruГ§Гөes**
   - O usuГЎrio iniciaria Claude manualmente depois
   - Ou: o script poderia usar **-p mode** para enviar uma mensagem inicial nГЈo-interativa

4. **E se usГЎssemos o modo `-p` (print)?**
   ```bash
   claude -p --append-system-prompt="$isolation_prompt" "Read progress.txt and features.json, then implement the next feature"
   ```
   - Isso roda uma Гәnica tarefa e sai
   - Problema: nГЈo Г© interativo, nГЈo permite iteraГ§ГЈo

5. **E se o script escrevesse um arquivo temporГЎrio que Claude lГӘ?**
   - Escrever `.claude-session-context.md` com o prompt
   - Usar hooks para injetar no contexto
   - Problema: complexidade extra

6. **Qual a abordagem mais KISS?**
   - Script prepara tudo e **apenas exibe** o que fazer
   - UsuГЎrio copia/cola o comando ou roda manualmente

Vou pesquisar se hГЎ outras formas de passar contexto para Claude:

Based on the search results, here's information about starting Claude Code CLI sessions with an initial prompt in non-interactive mode:

## Non-Interactive Mode (Headless Mode)

Claude Code includes headless mode for non-interactive contexts like CI, pre-commit hooks, build scripts, and automation. Use the `-p` flag with a prompt to enable headless mode, and `--output-format stream-json` for streaming JSON output.(no content)Note that headless mode does not persist between sessions.

## Starting a Session with an Initial Prompt

To begin a session, simply open your terminal in your project directory and type `claude`. You can also start a session with an initial prompt directly from the command line, for example, `claude "explain this project"`.

## Script-Friendly Non-Interactive Multi-Turn Sessions

For script-friendly usage, you can start once to capture a session_id, then chain every subsequent step with `--resume $sid`. The `--output-format json` includes a session_id field; you can also use `--no-interactive` with `--resume` for strictly non-interactive runs.

Example:
```bash
sid=$(claude -p "Start a refactor session: list the risk points" --output-format json | jq -r '.session_id')
```

Caution: some users report that repeatedly using `--continue` in non-interactive mode may create a new session, which looks like it resumed but actually changed IDs. For automation, prefer fixed session_id + `--resume`.

## Print Mode vs Interactive Mode

Currently, piping input to Claude Code is handled by the `--print` (`-p`) flag (e.g., `cat file.txt | claude -p "summarize this"`). This is extremely powerful for scripting and one-shot tasks. However, it's strictly non-interactive; the command executes, prints the output, and exits.

## Session Management Flags

The `--continue` flag automatically resumes the last active session stored in the SQLite database. The `--resume` flag presents an interactive selector with branch filtering and search capabilities.

input.prompt: Extract the feature request details about piping input to pre-populate interactive session prompt. What workarounds are suggested?

content.content:
# Feature Request: Piping Input to Pre-populate Interactive Session Prompt

## Overview
This GitHub issue (#6009) requests the ability to pipe input to Claude Code without the `-p` flag, which would launch an interactive TUI session with the piped content pre-populated in the prompt buffer.

## Current Problem
- The `--print` (`-p`) flag allows piping input but is **non-interactive** and exits after execution
- Users wanting interactive, multi-turn conversations with piped context must resort to cumbersome workarounds
- Current workflow breaks the natural command-line experience

## Proposed Solution

**User Experience:**
```bash
git diff main | claude
# The TUI launches with git diff content already in the prompt
# User types additional instructions and continues the conversation
```

1. User pipes content to `claude` without `-p` flag
2. Interactive TUI starts normally
3. Piped content appears pre-populated in the multiline input prompt
4. Cursor positioned at the end, ready for additional input
5. User can add instructions, edit, or submit as-is

## Suggested Workarounds

The issue discusses three alternatives to this requested feature:

### 1. **Using `claude -p`** (Current Method)
```bash
cat file.txt | claude -p "summarize this"
```
- вң… Great for scripting and one-shot tasks
- вқҢ Non-interactive; no follow-up questions possible

### 2. **Manual Copy & Paste**
- вң… Works for small content
- вқҢ Inefficient and error-prone for large files
- вқҢ Disrupts terminal workflow

### 3. **Temporary Files**
```bash
git diff > changes.txt
# Then in interactive session
@changes.txt
```
- вң… Functional approach
- вқҢ Adds extra steps and creates file clutter

## Use Cases

- **Interactive Code Review**: `git diff main | claude` вҶ’ ask follow-up questions
- **Log Analysis**: `tail -n 200 server.log | claude` вҶ’ investigate errors interactively
- **Code Comprehension**: `cat ./scripts/deploy.sh | claude` вҶ’ step-by-step explanation

## Additional Workaround Found in Comments

A user suggested using **shell command substitution** (with limitations):
```bash
claude "What is this text? '$(man git-diff | col -bx | head)' Next, count to 10."
```
- Limitations: Can hit `ARG_MAX` (~1-2MB), strips newlines

Usage: claude [options] [command] [prompt]

Claude Code - starts an interactive session by default, use -p/--print for

content.text:
Essa Г© uma ideia muito interessante! Criar um wrapper em Bun que:

1. Spawna o processo `claude`
2. Observa stdout/stderr para detectar:
   - Quando Claude fez uma pergunta (aguardando input)
   - Quando a tarefa terminou
   - Quando houve erro
3. Pode injetar comandos ou responder automaticamente

Deixe-me pesquisar como fazer isso:

Here are the search results for your query about Bun spawn interactive process with PTY, stdin, stdout, and bidirectional communication in 2025:

## Bun.spawn Overview

Bun allows you to spawn child processes with `Bun.spawn` or `Bun.spawnSync`.(no content)Under the hood, Bun.spawn and Bun.spawnSync use posix_spawn(3).

### Reading stdout

When using Bun.spawn(), the stdout of the child process can be consumed as a ReadableStream via proc.stdout.(no content)You can read results from the subprocess via the stdout and stderr properties. By default these are instances of ReadableStream.

### stdin Options

For stdin you may pass: `"ignore"`, `null`, `undefined` (the process will have no standard input - default), `"pipe"` (the process will have a new FileSink for standard input), `"inherit"` (the process will inherit the standard input of the current process), or `ArrayBufferView`, `Blob`, `Bun.file()`, `Response`, `Request` (the process will read from buffer/stream).

### stdout/stderr Options

For stdout and stdin you may pass: `"pipe"`, `undefined` (the process will have a ReadableStream for standard output/error), `"ignore"`, `null` (the process will have no standard output/error), `"inherit"` (the process will inherit the standard output/error of the current process), or `ArrayBufferView` (the process write to the preallocated buffer).

### Bidirectional Communication with ReadableStream

You can create a ReadableStream and pass it to stdin for input, while using "pipe" for stdout to capture output.

## PTY (Pseudo-Terminal) for Interactive Processes

If you need true PTY support for interactive processes, the search results indicate this is a different challenge:

Standard process pipes won't work for some programs because they only emit certain output when connected to a real TTY, and inheriting stdio bypasses your program entirely.(no content)"The genius of PTY is that it creates a bidirectional pipe that appears to be a real terminal to the spawned process."(no content)A PTY (Pseudo-Terminal) is a software-based virtual terminal that creates two connected endpoints: Controller endpoint (your program connects here) and Controlled endpoint.

For PTY support in JavaScript/Bun, you would typically need to use native bindings or a library like `node-pty` or `portable-pty` (for Rust), as Bun's built-in `Bun.spawn` uses standard pipes rather than pseudo-terminals.

### Performance Note

Bun's spawnSync spawns processes 60% faster than the Node.js child_process module.

Links: [{"title":"node-pty - npm","url":"https://www.npmjs.com/package/node-pty"},{"title":"GitHub - microsoft/node-pty: Fork pseudoterminals in Node.JS","url":"https://github.com/microsoft/node-pty"},{"title":"pty.node - npm","url":"https://www.npmjs.com/package/pty.node"},{"title":"Bun support В· Issue #632 В· microsoft/node-pty","url":"https://github.com/microsoft/node-pty/issues/632"},{"title":"How to use the node-pty.spawn function in node-pty | Snyk","url":"https://snyk.io/advisor/npm-package/node-pty/functions/node-pty.spawn"},{"title":"Top 5 node-pty Code Examples | Snyk","url":"https://snyk.io/advisor/npm-package/node-pty/example"},{"title":"node-pty beta not working as expected with bun build В· Issue #748 В· microsoft/node-pty","url":"https://github.com/microsoft/node-pty/issues/748"},{"title":"@zenyr/bun-pty 0.4.4 on npm - Libraries.io - security & maintenance data for open source software","url":"https://libraries.io/npm/@zenyr%2Fbun-pty"},{"title":"Remote terminal with Total.js + Node PTY + xTerm - Total.js Platform Blog","url":"https://blog.totaljs.com/posts/140289001cx61b/"},{"title":"Remote terminal with Total.js + Node PTY + xTerm - Bufferwall","url":"https://bufferwall.com/posts/140289001cx61b/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about node-pty and Bun.js compatibility for spawning interactive terminals:

## node-pty and Bun.js Compatibility

### The Problem
node-pty does not work with Bun. When trying to use it, users encounter errors like:(no content)`Cannot find module "../build/Debug/pty.node"` and `Cannot find module "../build/Release/pty.node"`(no content)Even when using node-pty's beta version to address nan/napi issues, users get a JavaScript crash in unixTerminal.js when trying to spawn the shell.(no content)The error is: `TypeError: this._socket.write is not a function`.

### Alternative: @zenyr/bun-pty

There's a Bun-specific alternative called **@zenyr/bun-pty**:

This is a cross-platform pseudo-terminal (PTY) implementation for Bun runtime, powered by Rust's portable-pty library and Bun's FFI capabilities.

Key features:
- This package uses bun:ffi and Bun-specific APIs. For Node.js, please use node-pty instead.
- It's cross-platform - works on macOS, Linux, and Windows with a clean Promise-based API similar to node-pty.
- This fork adds platform-specific packages (split native libraries into optional dependencies ~600KB vs ~3-4MB), ARM64 support for both Linux and macOS, fixed exit code handling, and corrected FFI bindings.
- The latest release was about 1 month ago (October 2025).

### node-pty Requirements (for Node.js)

Node.JS 16 or Electron 19 is required to use node-pty. What version of node is supported is currently mostly bound to whatever version Visual Studio Code is using.

**Summary**: If you need to spawn interactive terminals in Bun, you should use **@zenyr/bun-pty** (or the original **bun-pty**) instead of node-pty, as node-pty has compatibility issues with Bun's runtime.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.old_string:
# AnГЎlise: harness-package-01.sh - NГЈo hГЎ falha

## SituaГ§ГЈo Real

**O script estГЎ correto.** O "erro" que apareceu foi causado pela forma como testei:

```bash
./harness-package-01.sh 2>&1 | head -50  # вҶҗ ERRADO: pipe remove TTY
```

## Por que o erro apareceu

1. Quando uso `| head -50`, o stdout Г© redirecionado para um pipe
2. Pipes nГЈo sГЈo TTY (terminal)
3. Claude CLI detecta que nГЈo hГЎ TTY e assume modo `--print`
4. Modo `--print` requer uma query como argumento
5. Como nГЈo passei query, deu erro

## Comportamento Correto

Quando vocГӘ rodar diretamente no terminal:
```bash
./harness-package-01.sh  # вҶҗ CORRETO: terminal interativo
```

O Claude iniciarГЎ normalmente com o prompt de isolamento anexado.

## ConclusГЈo

**NГЈo hГЎ nada a corrigir.** O script funciona corretamente em uso real.

Eu (Claude) nГЈo consigo testar o comando interativo porque estou rodando dentro de outra sessГЈo Claude, que nГЈo tem TTY disponГӯvel para spawnar uma nova sessГЈo interativa.

## PrГіximo Passo

O usuГЎrio deve testar manualmente rodando `./harness-package-01.sh` diretamente no terminal.

    25вҶ’в”Ӯ  harness-runner.ts (Bun)                                    в”Ӯ
    26вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
    27вҶ’в”Ӯ  1. LГӘ features.json вҶ’ prГіxima tarefa                       в”Ӯ

    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’## ImplementaГ§ГЈo
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’### Arquivo: `scripts/harness-runner.ts`
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’```typescript
    45вҶ’import { spawn } from '@zenyr/bun-pty';
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’interface HarnessConfig {
    48вҶ’  packageDir: string;
    49вҶ’  featuresPath: string;
    50вҶ’  progressPath: string;
    51вҶ’  isolationPrompt: string;
    52вҶ’}
    53вҶ’
    54вҶ’async function runHarness(config: HarnessConfig) {
    55вҶ’  // 1. Ler prГіxima feature
    56вҶ’  const features = await Bun.file(config.featuresPath).json();
    57вҶ’  const next = features.features.find(f => !f.passes);
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’  if (!next) {
    60вҶ’    console.log('вң… All features complete!');
    61вҶ’    return;
    62вҶ’  }
    63вҶ’
    64вҶ’  console.log(`рҹҺҜ Starting: ${next.id} - ${next.description}`);
    65вҶ’
    66вҶ’  // 2. Spawnar Claude com PTY
    67вҶ’  const pty = spawn('claude', [
    68вҶ’    '--append-system-prompt', config.isolationPrompt
    69вҶ’  ], {
    70вҶ’    cwd: config.packageDir,
    71вҶ’    env: process.env
    72вҶ’  });
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’  // 3. Observar output
    75вҶ’  pty.onData((data) => {
    76вҶ’    process.stdout.write(data); // Exibir para usuГЎrio
    77вҶ’
    78вҶ’    // Detectar padrГөes
    79вҶ’    if (data.includes('Waiting for input')) {
    80вҶ’      // Claude estГЎ idle
    81вҶ’    }
    82вҶ’    if (data.includes('committed')) {
    83вҶ’      // Tarefa pode ter sido concluГӯda
    84вҶ’    }
    85вҶ’  });
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’  // 4. Passar input do usuГЎrio para Claude
    88вҶ’  process.stdin.on('data', (data) => {
    89вҶ’    pty.write(data.toString());
    90вҶ’  });
    91вҶ’
    92вҶ’  // 5. Aguardar tГ©rmino
    93вҶ’  pty.onExit(({ exitCode }) => {
    94вҶ’    console.log(`\nрҹ“Ҡ Session ended with code ${exitCode}`);
    95вҶ’    // Verificar se feature foi marcada como complete
    96вҶ’    // Se sim, perguntar se quer continuar com prГіxima
    97вҶ’  });
    98вҶ’}

   112вҶ’3. **AutomatizГЎvel** - Podemos encadear tarefas
   113вҶ’4. **TestГЎvel** - Podemos simular inputs
   114вҶ’

# AnГЎlise: harness-package-01.sh - NГЈo hГЎ falha

## SituaГ§ГЈo Real

**O script estГЎ correto.** O "erro" que apareceu foi causado pela forma como testei:

```bash
./harness-package-01.sh 2>&1 | head -50  # вҶҗ ERRADO: pipe remove TTY
```

## Por que o erro apareceu

1. Quando uso `| head -50`, o stdout Г© redirecionado para um pipe
2. Pipes nГЈo sГЈo TTY (terminal)
3. Claude CLI detecta que nГЈo hГЎ TTY e assume modo `--print`
4. Modo `--print` requer uma query como argumento
5. Como nГЈo passei query, deu erro

## Comportamento Correto

Quando vocГӘ rodar diretamente no terminal:
```bash
./harness-package-01.sh  # вҶҗ CORRETO: terminal interativo
```

O Claude iniciarГЎ normalmente com o prompt de isolamento anexado.

## ConclusГЈo

**NГЈo hГЎ nada a corrigir.** O script funciona corretamente em uso real.

Eu (Claude) nГЈo consigo testar o comando interativo porque estou rodando dentro de outra sessГЈo Claude, que nГЈo tem TTY disponГӯvel para spawnar uma nova sessГЈo interativa.

## PrГіximo Passo

O usuГЎrio deve testar manualmente rodando `./harness-package-01.sh` diretamente no terminal.

# Plano: Harness Runner com PTY (Bun)

## Requisitos (do usuГЎrio)

1. **LocalizaГ§ГЈo**: `scripts/harness-runner.ts`
2. **SeleГ§ГЈo**: Menu inicial para escolher qual harness (root, package-x, etc)
3. **Script npm**: Adicionar `"harness"` no package.json raiz
4. **AutomaГ§ГЈo**: Executar todas tarefas pendentes sequencialmente
5. **NotificaГ§Гөes macOS**: NotificaГ§ГЈo real quando:
   - Claude faz uma pergunta (precisa de input)
   - Todas as tarefas foram concluГӯdas

## Arquitetura

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  scripts/harness-runner.ts                                  в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ  1. Menu: Escolher harness (root, package-01, etc)          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  2. Loop: Para cada feature pendente                        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ     a. Spawna claude via PTY                                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ     b. Observa stdout para detectar estados                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ     c. Quando tarefa concluГӯda вҶ’ prГіxima                    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ     d. Se pergunta вҶ’ notifica macOS e aguarda               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  3. Fim: Notifica macOS "Todas tarefas concluГӯdas"          в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

## NotificaГ§Гөes macOS

```typescript
async function notify(title: string, message: string) {
  await Bun.spawn([
    'osascript', '-e',
    `display notification "${message}" with title "${title}" sound name "Ping"`
  ]);
}

// Uso:
await notify('рҹӨ– Harness', 'Claude precisa de input');
await notify('вң… Harness', 'Todas as tarefas concluГӯdas!');
```

## DetecГ§ГЈo de Estados

Observar stdout do Claude para detectar:

| PadrГЈo | Estado | AГ§ГЈo |
|--------|--------|------|
| `в•ӯв”Җ` ou prompt vazio por X segundos | Aguardando input | Notificar macOS |
| `git commit` | Tarefa concluГӯda | PrГіxima tarefa |
| `Error:` | Erro | Notificar + pausar |
| Exit code 0 | SessГЈo encerrada | Verificar features.json |

## Arquivos a Criar/Modificar

1. **`scripts/harness-runner.ts`** - Runner principal
2. **`scripts/harness-config.ts`** - ConfiguraГ§Гөes dos harnesses
3. **`package.json`** (raiz) - Adicionar script `"harness": "bun scripts/harness-runner.ts"`

## DependГӘncias

```bash
bun add @zenyr/bun-pty
```

## Fluxo do Menu

```
$ bun harness

рҹ”§ MMS Harness Runner
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Selecione o harness:

  1. рҹҸ  Root (apps/canvas) - 13 pendentes
  2. рҹ“Ұ harness-orchestrator - 4 pendentes
  3. рҹ“Ұ harness-dashboard - 32 pendentes

> 1

рҹҡҖ Iniciando Root harness...
рҹҺҜ Tarefa 1/13: F123 - Minimap navigation
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

[Claude session starts here...]
```

## Fontes

- [Bun.spawn docs](https://bun.sh/docs/api/spawn)
- [@zenyr/bun-pty](https://libraries.io/npm/@zenyr%2Fbun-pty)
- [macOS notifications via osascript](https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/AppleScript/Conceptual/AppleScriptLangGuide/)

## Requisitos (do usuГЎrio)

1. **LocalizaГ§ГЈo**: `scripts/harness-runner.ts`
2. **SeleГ§ГЈo**: Menu inicial para escolher qual harness (root, package-x, etc)
3. **Script npm**: Adicionar `"harness"` no package.json raiz
4. **AutomaГ§ГЈo**: Executar todas tarefas pendentes sequencialmente
5. **NotificaГ§Гөes macOS**: NotificaГ§ГЈo real quando:
   - Claude faz uma pergunta (precisa de input)
   - Todas as tarefas foram concluГӯdas

в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

## NotificaГ§Гөes macOS

```typescript
async function notify(title: string, message: string) {
  await Bun.spawn([
    'osascript', '-e',
    `display notification "${message}" with title "${title}" sound name "Ping"`
  ]);
}

// Uso:
await notify('рҹӨ– Harness', 'Claude precisa de input');
await notify('вң… Harness', 'Todas as tarefas concluГӯdas!');
```

## DetecГ§ГЈo de Estados

Observar stdout do Claude para detectar:

| PadrГЈo | Estado | AГ§ГЈo |
|--------|--------|------|
| `в•ӯв”Җ` ou prompt vazio por X segundos | Aguardando input | Notificar macOS |
| `git commit` | Tarefa concluГӯda | PrГіxima tarefa |
| `Error:` | Erro | Notificar + pausar |
| Exit code 0 | SessГЈo encerrada | Verificar features.json |

## Arquivos a Criar/Modificar

1. **`scripts/harness-runner.ts`** - Runner principal
2. **`scripts/harness-config.ts`** - ConfiguraГ§Гөes dos harnesses
3. **`package.json`** (raiz) - Adicionar script `"harness": "bun scripts/harness-runner.ts"`

## DependГӘncias

```bash
bun add @zenyr/bun-pty
```

```
$ bun harness

> 1

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
